steps.title: Use simple present for facts
steps.content: A zinc atom having 30 protons is a defining scientific fact, not a past event or temporary action. The verb should express a permanent property.

steps.title: Reject wrong tenses
steps.content: A suggests the atom acquired the property in the past. B places the fact only in the past. C makes it sound temporary or ongoing. The simple present form states the permanent property. The answer is D.
